name: anchorpy-fork
documentation:
# AnchorPy
--- [![Discord Chat](https://img.shields.io/discord/889577356681945098?color=blueviolet)](https://discord.gg/sxy4zxBckh) AnchorPy is the gateway to interacting with [Anchor](https://github.com/project-serum/anchor) programs in Python. It provides: - A static client generator - A dynamic client similar to `anchor-ts` - A Pytest plugin - A CLI with various utilities for Anchor Python development. Read the [Documentation](https://kevinheavey.github.io/anchorpy/). ## Installation (requires Python >=3.9) ```sh pip install anchorpy[cli, pytest] ``` Or, if you're not using the CLI or Pytest plugin features of AnchorPy you can just run `pip install anchorpy`. ### Development Setup If you want to contribute to AnchorPy, follow these steps to get set up: 1. Install [poetry](https://python-poetry.org/docs/#installation) 2. Install dev dependencies: ```sh poetry install ``` 3. Activate the poetry shell: ```sh poetry shell ```

functions.name: anchorpy.coder.common._sighash
documentation:
Not technically sighash, since we don't include the arguments.

(Because Rust doesn't allow function overloading.)

Args:

Returns:
    The sighash bytes.

functions.name: anchorpy.coder.common._type_size
documentation:
Return the size of the type in bytes.

For variable length types, just return 1.
Users should override this value in such cases.

Args:
    idl: The parsed `Idl` object.
    ty: The type object from the IDL.

Returns:
    The size of the object in bytes.

functions.name: anchorpy.coder.common._account_size
documentation:
Calculate account size in bytes.

Args:
    idl: The parsed `Idl` instance.
    idl_account: An item from `idl.accounts`.

Returns:
    Account size.

modules.name: anchorpy.workspace
documentation: This module contains code for creating the Anchor workspace.

functions.name: anchorpy.workspace.create_workspace
documentation:
Get a workspace from the provided path to the project root.

Args:
    path: The path to the project root. Defaults to the current working
        directory if omitted.
    url: The URL of the JSON RPC. Defaults to http://localhost:8899.

Returns:
    Mapping of program name to Program object.

functions.name: anchorpy.workspace.close_workspace
documentation:
Close the HTTP clients of all the programs in the workspace.

Args:
    workspace: The workspace to close.

Map an IDL typedef to a `Construct` object.

Raises:

functions.name: anchorpy.coder.idl._field_layout
documentation:
Map IDL spec to `borsh-construct` types.

Args:

    types: IDL type definitions.

Raises:

    ValueError: If the type is not found.
    ValueError: If the type is not implemented yet.

    `Construct` object from `borsh-construct`.

modules.name: anchorpy.pytest_plugin
documentation: This module provides the `localnet_fixture` fixture factory.

functions.name: anchorpy.pytest_plugin._fixed_xprocess
documentation: Yield session-scoped XProcess helper to manage long-running processes required for testing.

functions.name: anchorpy.pytest_plugin.localnet_fixture
documentation:
Create a fixture that sets up and tears down a localnet instance with workspace programs deployed.

Args:
    path: Path to root of the Anchor project.
    scope: Pytest fixture scope.
    timeout_seconds: Time to wait for Anchor localnet to start.
    build_cmd: Command to run before `anchor localnet`. Defaults to `anchor build`.

Returns:
    A localnet fixture for use with pytest.

functions.name: anchorpy.pytest_plugin.workspace_fixture
documentation:
Create a fixture that sets up and tears down a localnet instance and returns a workspace dict.

Equivalent to combining `localnet_fixture`, `create_workspace` and `close_workspace`.

Args:
    path: Path to root of the Anchor project.
    scope: Pytest fixture scope.
    timeout_seconds: Time to wait for Anchor localnet to start.
    build_cmd: Command to run before `anchor localnet`. Defaults to `anchor build`.

Returns:
    A workspace fixture for use with pytest.
Should figure out how to reuse localnet_fixture
instead of copy-pasting (Pytest didn't like it).

functions.name: anchorpy.pytest_plugin.bankrun_fixture
documentation:
Create a fixture that builds the project and starts a bankrun with all the programs in the workspace deployed.

Args:
    path: Path to root of the Anchor project.
    scope: Pytest fixture scope.
    build_cmd: Command to build the project. Defaults to `anchor build`.
    accounts: A sequence of (address, account_object) tuples, indicating
        what data to write to the given addresses.
    compute_max_units: Override the default compute unit limit for a transaction.
    transaction_account_lock_limit: Override the default transaction account lock limit.

Returns:
    A bankrun fixture for use with pytest.

methods.name: anchorpy.pytest_plugin._FixedXProcess.getinfo
documentation:
Return Process Info for the given external process.

Args:
    name: Name of the external process.

methods.name: anchorpy.pytest_plugin._FixedXProcess.ensure
documentation:
Return (PID, logfile) from a newly started or already running process.

Args:
    name: Name of the external process, used for caching info across test runs.
    preparefunc: A subclass of ProcessStarter.
    restart: Force restarting the process if it is running.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: If process fails to start within the required time.

Returns:
    (PID, logfile) logfile will be seeked to the end if the
    server was running, otherwise seeked to the line after
    where the wait pattern matched.

functions.name: anchorpy.cli.callback
documentation: AnchorPy CLI.

functions.name: anchorpy.cli.shell
documentation:
Start IPython shell with AnchorPy workspace object initialized.

Note that you should run `anchor localnet` before `anchorpy shell`.

functions.name: anchorpy.cli.init
documentation:
Create a basic Python test file for an Anchor program.

This does not replace `anchor init`, but rather should be
run after it.

The test file will live at `tests/test_$PROGRAM_NAME.py`.

Init.

methods.name: anchorpy.program.namespace.account.AccountClient.fetch
documentation:
Return a deserialized account.

Args:
    address: The address of the account to fetch.
    commitment: Bank state to query.


Raises:
    AccountDoesNotExistError: If the account doesn't exist.
    AccountInvalidDiscriminator: If the discriminator doesn't match the IDL.

methods.name: anchorpy.program.namespace.account.AccountClient.fetch_multiple
documentation:
Return multiple deserialized accounts.

Accounts not found or with wrong discriminator are returned as None.

Args:
    addresses: The addresses of the accounts to fetch.
    batch_size: The number of `getMultipleAccounts` objects to send
        in each HTTP request.
    commitment: Bank state to query.

methods.name: anchorpy.program.namespace.account.AccountClient.create_instruction
documentation:
Return an instruction for creating this account.

Args:

    size_override: Optional override for the account size. Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    The instruction to create the account.

methods.name: anchorpy.program.namespace.account.AccountClient.all
documentation:
Return all instances of this account type for the program.

Args:
    buffer: bytes filter to append to the discriminator.
    filters: (optional) Options to compare a provided series of bytes with
        program account data at a particular offset.
        Note: an int entry is converted to a `dataSize` filter.

methods.name: anchorpy.program.namespace.account.AccountClient.size

Provides methods for fetching and creating accounts.

modules.name: anchorpy.program.namespace.simulate
documentation: This module contains code for creating simulate functions.

functions.name: anchorpy.program.namespace.simulate._build_simulate_item
documentation:
Build the function to simulate transactions for a given method of a program.

Args:
    idl_ix: An IDL instruction object.
    tx_fn: The function to generate the `Transaction` object.
    idl_errors: Mapping of error code to message.
    provider: A provider instance.
    coder: The program's coder object.
    program_id: The program ID.
    idl: The parsed Idl instance.

Returns:
    The simulate function.

classes.name: anchorpy.program.namespace.simulate.SimulateResponse
documentation: The result of a simulate function call.

classes.name: anchorpy.program.namespace.simulate._SimulateFn

methods.name: anchorpy.program.namespace.simulate._SimulateFn.__call__
documentation:
Protocol definition.

Args:
    *args: The positional arguments for the program. The type and number
        of these arguments depend on the program being used.
    ctx: non-argument parameters to pass to the method.

A single method generated from an IDL.

It simulates a method against a cluster configured by the provider,
returning a list of all the events and raw logs that were emitted
during the execution of the method.

Build the function that sends transactions for the given method.

classes.name: anchorpy.program.namespace.rpc._RpcFn

methods.name: anchorpy.program.namespace.rpc._RpcFn.__call__
documentation:
Call the function (this is just a protocol declaration).

Args:
    *args: The positional arguments for the program. The type and number
        of these arguments depend on the program being used.
    ctx: non-argument parameters to pass to the method.
_RpcFn is a single RPC method generated from an IDL, sending a transaction paid for and signed by the configured provider.

Build the function that generates Transaction objects.

Make sure that the function looks like this.

Args:
    *args: The positional arguments for the program. The type and number
        of these arguments depend on the program being used.
    payer: The transaction fee payer.
    blockhash: A recent blockhash.
    ctx: non-argument parameters to pass to the method.
A function to create a `Transaction` for a given program instruction.

modules.name: anchorpy.program.context
documentation: This module contains code handling the Context object.

functions.name: anchorpy.program.context._check_args_length
documentation:
Check that the correct number of args is passed to the RPC function.

Args:
    idl_ix: The IDL instruction object.
    args: The instruction arguments.

Raises:
    ArgsError: If the correct number of args is not parsed.

classes.name: anchorpy.program.context.Context
documentation:
Context provides all non-argument inputs for generating Anchor transactions.

Attributes:
    accounts: The accounts used in the instruction context.
    remaining_accounts: All accounts to pass into an instruction *after* the main
    `accounts`. This can be used for optional or otherwise unknown accounts.
    signers: Accounts that must sign a given transaction.
    pre_instructions: Instructions to run *before* a given method. Often this is
        used, for example to create accounts prior to executing a method.
    post_instructions: Instructions to run *after* a given method. Often this is
        used, for example to close accounts prior to executing a method.
    options: Commitment parameters to use for a transaction.

methods.name: anchorpy.program.event.EventParser.parse_logs
documentation:
Parse a list of logs using a provided callback.

Args:
    logs: The logs to parse.
    callback: The function to handle the parsed log.

methods.name: anchorpy.program.event.EventParser.handle_log
documentation:
Main log handler.

Args:
    execution: The execution stack.
    log: log string from the RPC node.

Returns:
    A three element array of the event, the next program
    that was invoked for CPI, and a boolean indicating if
    a program has completed execution (and thus should be popped off the
    execution stack).

methods.name: anchorpy.program.event.EventParser.handle_program_log
documentation:
Handle logs from *this* program.

Args:
    log: log string from the RPC node.

methods.name: anchorpy.program.event.EventParser.handle_system_log
documentation:
Handle logs when the current program being executing is *not* this.

Args:
    log: log string from the RPC node.
Parser to handle on_logs callbacks.

methods.name: anchorpy.program.core.Program.close
documentation: Use this when you are done with the client.

methods.name: anchorpy.program.core.Program.fetch_raw_idl
documentation:
Fetch an idl from the blockchain as a raw JSON dictionary.

Args:
    address: The program ID.
    provider: The network and wallet context.

Raises:
    IdlNotFoundError: If the requested IDL account does not exist.

Returns:
    str: The raw IDL.

methods.name: anchorpy.program.core.Program.fetch_idl
documentation:
Fetch and parse an idl from the blockchain.

Args:
    address: The program ID.
    provider: The network and wallet context.

Returns:
    Idl: The fetched IDL.

methods.name: anchorpy.program.core.Program.at
documentation:
Generate a Program client by fetching the IDL from the network.

In order to use this method, an IDL must have been previously initialized
via the anchor CLI's `anchor idl init` command.

Args:
    address: The program ID.
    provider: The network and wallet context.

Returns:
    The Program instantiated using the fetched IDL.

Program provides the IDL deserialized client representation of an Anchor program.

This API is the one stop shop for all things related to communicating with
on-chain programs. Among other things, one can send transactions, fetch
deserialized accounts, decode instruction data, subscribe to account
changes, and listen to events.

In addition to field accessors and methods, the object provides a set of
dynamically generated properties, also known as namespaces, that
map one-to-one to program methods and accounts.

modules.name: anchorpy.utils.rpc
documentation: This module contains the invoke function.

functions.name: anchorpy.utils.rpc.invoke
documentation:
Send a transaction to a program with the given accounts and instruction data.

Args:
    program_id: The program ID
    provider: the `Provider` instance.
    accounts: `AccountMeta` objects.
    data: The transaction data.

Returns:
    The transaction signature.

functions.name: anchorpy.utils.rpc.get_multiple_accounts
documentation:
Fetch multiple account infos through batched `getMultipleAccount` RPC requests.

Args:
    connection: The `solana-py` client object.
    pubkeys: Pubkeys to fetch.
    batch_size: The number of `getMultipleAccount` objects to include in each
        HTTP request.
    commitment: Bank state to query.

Returns:
    Account infos and pubkeys.

classes.name: anchorpy.utils.rpc.AccountInfo
documentation:
Information describing an account.

Attributes:
    executable: `True` if this account's data contains a loaded program.
    owner: Identifier of the program that owns the account.
    lamports: Number of lamports assigned to the account.
    data: Optional data assigned to the account.
    rent_epoch: Optional rent epoch info for for account.

modules.name: anchorpy.utils.token
documentation: This module contains utilities for the SPL Token Program.

functions.name: anchorpy.utils.token.create_token_account
documentation:
Create a token account.

Args:
    prov: An anchorpy Provider instance.
    mint: The pubkey of the token's mint.
    owner: User account that will own the new account.

Returns:
    The pubkey of the new account.

functions.name: anchorpy.utils.token.create_token_account_instrs
documentation:
Generate instructions for creating a token account.

Args:
    provider: An anchorpy Provider instance.
    new_account_pubkey: The pubkey of the new account.
    mint: The pubkey of the token's mint.
    owner: User account that will own the new account.

Returns:
    Transaction instructions to create the new account.

functions.name: anchorpy.utils.token.create_mint_and_vault
documentation:
Create a mint and a vault, then mint tokens to the vault.

Args:
    provider: An anchorpy Provider instance.
    amount: The amount of tokens to mint to the vault.
    owner: User account that will own the new account.
    decimals: The number of decimal places for the token to support.

Returns:
    The mint and vault pubkeys.

functions.name: anchorpy.utils.token.parse_token_account
documentation:
Parse `AccountInfo` from RPC response.

Args:
    info: the `get_account_info` RPC response.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the fetched data is the wrong size.
    AttributeError: If the account is not owned by the token program.

Returns:
    The parsed `AccountInfo`.

functions.name: anchorpy.utils.token.get_token_account
documentation:
Retrieve token account information.

Args:
    provider: The anchorpy Provider instance.
    addr: The pubkey of the token account.

Returns:
    The parsed `AccountInfo` of the token account.

functions.name: anchorpy.utils.token.get_mint_info
documentation:
Retrieve mint information.

Args:
    provider: The anchorpy Provider instance.
    addr: The pubkey of the mint.

Returns:
    The parsed `MintInfo`.

functions.name: anchorpy.utils.token.parse_mint_account
documentation:
Parse raw RPC response into `MintInfo`.

Args:
    info: The RPC response from calling `.get_account_info` for the mint pubkey.

Raises:
    AttributeError: If the account is not owned by the Token Program.
    ValueError: If the fetched data is the wrong size.

Returns:
    The parsed `MintInfo`.

modules.name: anchorpy.provider
documentation: This module contains the Provider class and associated utilities.

classes.name: anchorpy.provider.Provider

methods.name: anchorpy.provider.Provider.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Provider.

Args:
    connection: The cluster connection where the program is deployed.
    wallet: The wallet used to pay for and sign all transactions.
    opts: Transaction confirmation options to use by default.

methods.name: anchorpy.provider.Provider.local
documentation:
Create a `Provider` with a wallet read from the local filesystem.

Args:
    url: The network cluster url.
    opts: The default transaction confirmation options.

methods.name: anchorpy.provider.Provider.readonly
documentation:
Create a `Provider` that can only fetch data, not send transactions.

Args:
    url: The network cluster url.
    opts: The default transaction confirmation options.

methods.name: anchorpy.provider.Provider.env
documentation: Create a `Provider` using the `ANCHOR_PROVIDER_URL` environment variable.

methods.name: anchorpy.provider.Provider.simulate
documentation:
Simulate the given transaction, returning emitted logs from execution.

Args:
    tx: The transaction to send.
    signers: The set of signers in addition to the provider wallet that will
        sign the transaction.
    opts: Transaction confirmation options.

Returns:
    The transaction simulation result.

methods.name: anchorpy.provider.Provider.send
documentation:
Send the given transaction, paid for and signed by the provider's wallet.

Args:
    tx: The transaction to send.
    signers: The set of signers in addition to the provider wallet that will
        sign the transaction.
    opts: Transaction confirmation options.

Returns:
    The transaction signature from the RPC server.

methods.name: anchorpy.provider.Provider.send_all
documentation:
Similar to `send`, but for an array of transactions and signers.

Args:
    txs: a list of transaction objects.
    opts: Transaction confirmation options.

Returns:
    The transaction signatures from the RPC server.

methods.name: anchorpy.provider.Provider.__aenter__
documentation: Use as a context manager.

methods.name: anchorpy.provider.Provider.__aexit__
documentation: Exit the context manager.

methods.name: anchorpy.provider.Provider.close
documentation: Use this when you are done with the connection.
The network and wallet context used to send transactions paid for and signed by the provider.

classes.name: anchorpy.provider.Wallet

methods.name: anchorpy.provider.Wallet.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the wallet.

Args:
    payer: the Keypair used to sign transactions.

methods.name: anchorpy.provider.Wallet.public_key
documentation: Get the public key of the wallet.

methods.name: anchorpy.provider.Wallet.sign_transaction
documentation:
Sign a transaction using the wallet's keypair.

Args:
    tx: The transaction to sign.

Returns:
    The signed transaction.

methods.name: anchorpy.provider.Wallet.sign_all_transactions
documentation:
Sign a list of transactions using the wallet's keypair.

Args:
    txs: The transactions to sign.

Returns:
    The signed transactions.

methods.name: anchorpy.provider.Wallet.local
documentation:
Create a wallet instance from the filesystem.

Uses the path at the ANCHOR_WALLET env var if set,
otherwise uses ~/.config/solana/id.json.

methods.name: anchorpy.provider.Wallet.dummy
documentation: Create a dummy wallet instance that won't be used to sign transactions.
Python wallet object.
